title: 🎙️ Wild Spaces & Outdoor Life
description: Today we head into the wild — the Chilcotin plateau, river valleys, and backcountry that define the Cariboo experience. We're covering the technology helping monitor, protect, and navigate these landscapes: wildfire detection, wildlife tracking, satellite communication, and the tools keeping backcountry travellers safe and conservationists informed. - Daily themed article selection for podcast generation

_podcast.theme_description: Today we head into the wild — the Chilcotin plateau, river valleys, and backcountry that define the Cariboo experience. We're covering the technology helping monitor, protect, and navigate these landscapes: wildfire detection, wildlife tracking, satellite communication, and the tools keeping backcountry travellers safe and conservationists informed.
_podcast.theme_scoring_prompt: Rate how well this article fits the theme 'Wild Spaces & Outdoor Life' through a RURAL TECH AND INNOVATION lens. Strong fits include: wildfire detection tech and BC Wildfire monitoring systems, satellite/drone surveillance for Chilcotin wilderness, wildlife tracking and conservation technology, watershed sensors and salmon habitat monitoring, weather stations and climate tracking in backcountry, avalanche forecasting tech, trail mapping and GPS systems, outdoor recreation apps and connectivity, backcountry communication devices (satellite messengers, Meshtastic), flood/drought prediction systems, remote cameras for wildlife research, and how technology helps manage and protect Cariboo wild spaces. Prefer articles that bridge wilderness/conservation AND technology. Score 0-100 where 100 is a perfect thematic match and 0 is completely unrelated.

items.title: [The Guardian Global Development] Rainwater harvesting and eco-gardens: how one Colombian neighbourhood helped a whole city plan for climate change
items.content_html:
From rainwater harvesting to tree nurseries, communities in Medellín are taking steps to increase their landslide and flooding resilience

In his home on a steep hillside in the neighbourhood of Golondrinas in Medellín, Róbinson Velásquez Cartagena stands proudly next to two large tanks of water – a rainwater harvesting system he designed and built to help reduce the risk of flooding and landslides.

It is one of the nature-based solutions that Velásquez and others in the community have proposed as part of a disaster risk and climate crisis adaptation plan for Comuna 8, a growing informal settlement of 150,000 people in Colombia’s second-largest city.

Continue reading...

items.title: [New Atlas] Low-protein diet shifts fat tissue into burn mode
items.content_html:
Not all body fat is created equal. There is white adipose tissue, which stores excess energy, brown adipose tissue, which burns energy, and a third category known as “beige” fat. This type of fat can emerge from white fat under certain conditions and take on the energy-burning properties of brown fat, making it a compelling target for obesity research.

Continue Reading

Category: Obesity, Illnesses and conditions, Body and Mind

Tags: Weight Loss, Weight, Diet, Protein, City of Hope, McMaster University, Keio University

items.title: [Modern Farmer] On a Maine Farm, Regeneration Starts With the Sheep
items.content_html:
What do sheep, regeneration of Maine farmland, and indigenous knowledge have to do with one another? Quite a bit, when it comes to Melissa Schneider, shepherd and landowner of Casa Portal. Along with herding and sustainable land management, Melissa is one of many women farmers who are staking their place.

The post On a Maine Farm, Regeneration Starts With the Sheep appeared first on Modern Farmer.
